channel.title: HEBBS Blog
channel.description: Engineering insights, product updates, and deep dives from the HEBBS team.

item.title: Stop Building Opinionated Memory
item.description: Every agent memory system picks one retrieval strategy and optimizes for one benchmark. But in production, the same agent needs to recall differently for every query. The right answer is to stop deciding for the agent and let it tune retrieval itself.

item.title: The Memory Palace Comes to the Homepage
item.description: HEBBS now has an interactive force-directed memory graph on the homepage, a new agent-control section showing every tunable parameter, and a sharper identity: memory that wires itself.

item.title: How HEBBS Works: From Markdown to Memory Palace
item.description: The complete technical walkthrough. Two-phase ingest pipeline, RocksDB storage schema with eight column families, four recall strategies with documented complexity, and how an AI agent uses HEBBS end-to-end in a real conversation loop.

item.title: One Binary. One Brain.
item.description: HEBBS used to ship two binaries that stored memories in two separate places. They could not see each other. v0.3.0 fixes that: one binary, one RocksDB, one recall that searches everything.

item.title: Reflect and Contradict: How HEBBS Consolidates Knowledge Without an LLM
item.description: Two pipelines that make memory smarter over time. Reflection turns episodes into insights. Contradiction detection catches conflicting beliefs. Both work with or without an LLM.

item.title: Your Agent Is Asking the Wrong Questions
item.description: Raw conversational queries score 0.49 relevance against a retrieval model. Rewritten cues score 0.87. The difference is the agent's intelligence layer: query rewriting, strategy selection, and weight tuning.

item.title: How HEBBS Scores Memories, And Why Repeated Patterns Matter More
item.description: A deep dive into how HEBBS ranks, reinforces, and decays memories. Plus: why insights from larger clusters now score higher, and the cognitive science behind it.

item.title: Your Agent's Files Just Got Smarter
item.description: HEBBS now sits next to your markdown files as an invisible intelligence layer. No migration, no database imports. Your files stay yours. HEBBS makes them searchable, connected, and self-organizing.

item.title: Hello World: Introducing the HEBBS Blog
item.description: Welcome to the HEBBS Blog. We're building the memory engine for AI agents, and here's what we'll be writing about.

item.title: Give Your AI Agent a Memory That Actually Works
item.description: AI agents forget everything between sessions. HEBBS is a local-first cognitive memory engine that stores, recalls, reflects, and forgets knowledge so your agent never asks the same question twice.
